key_insight: MgHCu3 remains structurally and electronically robust, but its phonons and predicted superconducting Tc are highly sensitive to computational setup, challenging the earlier 42 K claim.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that superconductivity predictions for hydride perovskites must be benchmarked across functionals and k-point/phonon grids before being trusted for materials screening.
method: Recomputed crystal and electronic structure with PBEsol and PBE.
Evaluated lattice dynamics and phonon dispersion using different grid densities.
Calculated electron-phonon coupling and Tc under multiple Gaussian broadenings.
evidence_signal: Across settings, the computed critical temperature varied widely from about 10 K to 31 K, and never exceeded the classical 40 K limit.
watch_item: Predicted superconducting properties are highly sensitive to numerical resolution and functional choice, so single-setup Tc claims may be unreliable.
